description:
You are an energy auditor reviewing a small commercial building design in Energy3D. The initial model uses generic materials that fail local energy codes. Your task is to update the thermal envelope to meet high-performance specifications and run an energy simulation.

Expected Actions:
1. Open the existing file: ~/Documents/Energy3D/building-shape.ng3
2. Change the project's geographic location/city to Boston, MA (via the map or location settings).
3. Modify the properties of the architectural elements using the properties panel to meet the new code:
   - All Walls: Set U-value to 0.25
   - Roof: Set U-value to 0.15
   - Roof: Set Albedo to 0.65
   - All Windows: Set U-value to 1.20
4. Save the modified project as ~/Documents/Energy3D/compliant_boston_building.ng3
5. Run the Annual Building Energy Analysis (Analysis menu > Buildings > Annual Energy Analysis).
6. Save a picture of the resulting energy analysis graph to ~/Documents/Energy3D/energy_results.png (you may use a screenshot tool like 'scrot' or the graph's export feature).
